subject: Product Management
title: The Cost of Skipping Discovery
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Cost of Skipping Discovery usable.

hook.text: A feature that takes three months to build and nobody uses costs far more than the engineering hours.

buildup.text: There's the direct cost: salaries, infrastructure, opportunity cost of what the team didn't build instead.

discovery.text: Then the hidden cost: team morale drops when their work is ignored. Talented people leave for teams that ship impact.

twist.text: A two-week discovery sprint costs 10% of a three-month build. It can prevent 100% of the wasted effort.

climax.text: Discovery isn't a luxury. It's insurance against the most expensive mistake in product: building the wrong thing.

punchline.text: Two weeks of learning beats three months of waste.

quiz.question: What is the hidden cost of skipping product discovery?
options: Wasted engineering time, low morale, and talented people leaving
Discovery always costs more than building
There are no costs to skipping discovery
